answer: Viṣṇu Smṛti chapter 22 (work slug: unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8) discusses āśauca — ritual impurity arising from birth and death. It covers: (1) the duration of āśauca periods for each varṇa (10 days for Brāhmaṇas, 12 for Rājanyas, 15 for Vaiśyas, 30 for Śūdras); (2) what activities are suspended during āśauca (sacrifices, gift-giving, Vedic study); (3) penances for eating another's āśauca food, varying by the caste combination involved; (4) special cases such as miscarriage, stillbirth, infant death, married women, overlapping impurities, and learning of a death while abroad; (5) categories exempt from āśauca (those killed in battle, by the king, etc.; kings on royal duty; those under a vow; artisans at work); and (6) general bodily purification rules — bathing requirements after contact with corpses, menstruating women, Caṇḍālas, etc., closing with verses on the twelve bodily impurities and the hierarchy of purifiers.
